name: AscendEX (BitMax) Crypto Exchange API
what_it_does: Singapore-based cryptocurrency exchange (formerly BitMax) with REST and WebSocket API — enabling traders and developers to access spot trading, perpetual futures, margin trading, staking, market data, and account management, with HMAC-SHA256 authentication and a well-documented API with multiple language examples.
use_cases: Executing spot and perpetual futures via AscendEX API for algorithmic trading strategies
Accessing market data via AscendEX API for cryptocurrency price feeds and order book depth
Managing cross-margin and isolated-margin positions via AscendEX API for leveraged trading
Streaming real-time data via AscendEX WebSocket for live order book and trade feeds
Fetching account balances via AscendEX API for multi-exchange portfolio management
best_when: A developer needs a mid-tier exchange with good API documentation, staking support, and perpetual futures alongside spot trading, as an alternative to top-tier exchanges.
avoid_when: You need US market access, top-tier liquidity, or institutional-grade infrastructure.

auth.notes: HMAC-SHA256 signature. Headers: x-auth-key, x-auth-signature, x-auth-timestamp. Signature: HMAC-SHA256 of timestamp + 'BM' + request path. Note: signature uses request path only (NOT full URL, NOT body). x-auth-timestamp: Unix milliseconds. API key from AscendEX account. Three-level authentication: 0 (public), 1 (account read), 2 (trading). REST API at ascendex.com/api/pro/v1/. WebSocket: wss://ascendex.com/{accountGroup}/api/pro/v1/websocket-for-hft. AccountGroup: numeric prefix in API paths.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; spot trading: 0.1% maker/0.1% taker standard
pricing.paid_tiers: ASD token for fee discounts; VIP tiers by volume
pricing.notes: Singaporean company. Rebranded from BitMax to AscendEX in 2021. Founded 2018. ASD token: native exchange token for fee discounts. Products: Spot, Margin (cross + isolated), Perpetual futures, Cash & Carry. Staking: liquid staking for PoS assets. AccountGroup: unique numeric prefix per user (e.g., /1/api/pro/v1/) — required for all authenticated calls. HMAC signature signs only the path (not body) — unusual vs other exchanges. Good API documentation on GitHub. Multiple code examples (Python, JS, Java). US-restricted. ~200+ trading pairs.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; enforced per account

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: x-auth-key/signature/timestamp headers. Signature: ONLY path (not body). AccountGroup prefix required in all URLs. Rebranded (BitMax→AscendEX). US-restricted. ASD token. Three auth levels.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET requests idempotent. Order placement: use coid (client order ID) for idempotent tracking. Market data reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: HMAC signature covers ONLY the path (not body, not query params) — different from most exchanges
AccountGroup: every authenticated user has a numeric prefix (get via GET /api/pro/v1/info) — required in all private URLs
Formerly BitMax — documentation may reference both names; same platform
Three auth levels (0/1/2): ensure API key has correct permission level for operations
US-restricted exchange — verify access from your jurisdiction before production integration

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC-SHA256 path-only signature — simpler but slightly less secure (body not included in signature). Three permission levels (public/read/trading) — good access segmentation. Singaporean company: MAS regulatory environment. AccountGroup per user adds user isolation. US-restricted. x-auth-key in header (not query param). API key and secret require secure storage.
